title: User equipment positioning in long-term evolution coordinated multipoint communication systems
abstract: Embodiments of the present disclosure describe systems and methods for UE positioning in wireless networks. Various embodiments may include signaling of virtual identifiers associated with remote radio heads of a coordinated multipoint communication system and generating a positioning reference signal based on the virtual identifiers. Other embodiments may be described and/or claimed.
claims: 1. A method comprising: sending, by a base station in a wireless communications network to a server in the wireless communications network, location assistance data comprising a physical cell identifier of a cell associated with the base station and individual identifiers of one or more transmission points (TPs) in the cell, the individual identifiers identifying each TP from other TPs associated with the cell; based on sending the location assistance data to the server, receiving, by the base station from the server, a positioning protocol message that is generated by a positioning server in the wireless communications network and forwarded by the positioning server to a mobility management entity (MME) in the wireless communications network, wherein the positioning protocol message includes an observed time difference of arrival (OTDOA) cell information list data information element, the information element (IE) including the physical cell identifier of the cell and the individual identifiers of the one or more TPs in the cell; sending, by the base station, the positioning protocol message to a user equipment (UE) in the wireless communications network; and receiving, by the base station, positioning measurement data from the UE, wherein the positioning measurement data is generated by the UE using one or more of a positioning reference signal (PRS) received from a first TP of the one or more TPs, the physical cell identifier of the cell obtained from the IE, or an individual identifier of the first TP obtained from the IE.
